workflow.name: Generate Instagram posts with OpenAI, RSS news, and auto image posting
workflow.description:
## What this workflow does

This workflow automatically generates and publishes Instagram content using AI.

It:
- Generates content ideas using AI
- Creates images automatically
- Edits images (resize/format)
- Publishes directly to Instagram

## How it works

1. Trigger starts the workflow (manual or scheduled)
2. AI generates a content idea and prompt
3. Image is generated via API
4. Image is processed and formatted
5. Post is published automatically

## Setup

- Connect your OpenAI (or AI provider) credentials
- Add your image generation API key
- Connect your Instagram account
- (Optional) Connect Google Drive for storage

Setup time: 15–20 minutes

parameters.text:
=You are a social media content strategist and visual content creator for Instagram.

Your task is to transform ONE real RSS news item into a useful Instagram post.

You must return:

1. "prompt_image": a prompt to generate one vertical image for Instagram (1080x1350)
2. "caption": a short Instagram caption in Spanish

Available information:
- News title: {{ $json.title }}
- News summary: {{ $json.contentSnippet }}
- News link: {{ $json.link }}
- Category: {{ $('CODE TEMAS').item.json.categoria }}
- Editorial focus: {{ $('CODE TEMAS').item.json.enfoque }}

Brand direction:
- Audience: {{ $('CODE TEMAS').item.json.audience }}
- Brand style: {{ $('CODE TEMAS').item.json.brand_style }}
- Visual direction: {{ $('CODE TEMAS').item.json.visual_direction }}

Goal:
Turn a real news story into a visual content piece that is clear, relevant, and useful for entrepreneurs, brands, or businesses.

Rules for "prompt_image":
- Describe a concrete and visually clear scene
- The scene must relate directly to the real news story
- Avoid generic office scenes and abstract AI clichés
- The image should feel modern, clean, and professional
- The image must reflect the defined brand direction
- It should feel aligned with the intended audience and visual style, not generic
- Format must be vertical 1080x1350
- No real logos
- No watermarks
- No excessive text inside the image
- If text is included, it must be short, natural, and easy to read

Rules for "caption":
- Write in Spanish
- Maximum 450 characters
- Explain the impact, opportunity, or lesson of the news for businesses
- Keep it natural and professional
- Maximum 3 hashtags
- No emojis
- Do not copy the original headline literally

Return ONLY valid JSON with this exact structure:

{
  "prompt_image": "string",
  "caption": "string"
}

nodes.name: Send message and wait for response1

parameters.content:
## News sources

This workflow uses a rotating list of RSS feeds grouped by topic.

**What happens here:**
- one topic is selected for the current run
- one matching RSS feed is used
- the latest news item is extracted

**You can customize:**
- RSS feed URLs
- content themes
- editorial focus

**Result:** a fresh content angle without manual research.

## AI prompt generation

This block transforms news into social media content.

**What the AI creates:**
- an image generation prompt
- a short Instagram caption

**Goal:**
Turn real information into engaging, usable content.

**Result:** structured input ready for image generation.

## Image generation

This block creates the **final Instagram-ready image**.

**What happens here:**
- AI output is used as input
- Image is generated via API
- Response is converted into binary
- Image is resized to the final format

**Result:** a clean, ready-to-use visual asset.

## Image hosting (required)

This step uploads the generated image to cloud storage to create a public URL.

**Why this is required:**
- Instagram API requires a public image URL
- It does NOT accept binary files directly

**What happens here:**
- the generated image is uploaded
- a public URL is created
- that URL is used in the publish step

**You must configure:**
- a storage provider (S3, or similar)
- public access to the file or link

**Result:** a valid public image URL that Instagram can use.

## Approval step

Before publishing, content is sent for manual review.

**This helps validate:**
- image quality
- caption clarity
- brand alignment

**Result:** better control before going live.

## Publish

Once approved, the workflow publishes the content to Instagram automatically.

**What happens:**
- image is posted
- caption is included
- no manual publishing required
















**Important:**
Replace all credentials and IDs before using.
The publish step expects a field called: "public_image_url"

Make sure your upload node returns this field.

**Result:** fully automated social media publishing.

## Setup

To use this workflow:

1. Connect your AI provider (OpenAI or Google Gemini)
2. Add your image generation API key
3. Connect your Instagram account
4. Configure image hosting (S3)
   → The image must be publicly accessible via URL5. 
5. Replace any placeholder values in the HTTP node

Estimated setup time: 10–20 minutes

## Trigger

This workflow runs automatically on a schedule.

**What it does:**
- Generates new Instagram content periodically
- Requires no manual input once configured

**You can adjust:**
- frequency (daily, weekly, etc.)

**Result:** continuous content generation without manual effort.

## Content direction

This workflow also defines a simple creative direction for the generated post.

**You can customize:**
- audience
- brand style
- visual direction

These settings help the AI create images that feel more aligned with your brand instead of looking generic.

data.details:
The Edit Image node allows you to manipulate and edit images. Use this node when you want to:

- Blur an image
- Add a border to an image
- Create a new image
- Crop an image
- Composite an image on top of another
- Draw on an image
- Get information about the image
- Rotate an image
- Change the size of an image
- Shear an image along the X or Y axis
- Add text to the image

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
